summary: Matched HSD_ByteCodeEval in sysdolphin/baselib/bytecode.c through small codegen-sensitive rewrites, local type/signature cleanup, and synchronized prototypes in bytecode.h and robj.c. The decomp.dev report confirmed HSD_ByteCodeEval moved from 99.92% to 100.00% and the overall GALE01 matched-code metric increased by 5640 bytes.

key_files.role: Main match work for HSD_ByteCodeEval, including fmodf rewrite, local type adjustments, stack operand lifetime changes, atan2 branch cleanup, and boolean-expression simplification.
Updated HSD_ByteCodeEval declaration from float*/int to f32*/s32.
Updated the local extern declaration of HSD_ByteCodeEval used near expEvaluate to match the f32*/s32 signature.

what_changed: Changed HSD_ByteCodeEval signature from float HSD_ByteCodeEval(u8* bytecode, float* args, int nb_args) to float HSD_ByteCodeEval(u8* bytecode, f32* args, s32 nb_args).
Synchronized the HSD_ByteCodeEval prototype in bytecode.h and the extern declaration in robj.c to use f32* args and s32 nb_args.

Rewrote the local static inline fmodf helper from a one-line ternary into an explicit f32 fmodf(f32 divisor, f32 dividend) with a long long quotient temporary and early return when fabs(divisor) > fabs(dividend).
Adjusted HSD_ByteCodeEval local declarations: split HSD_SList* stack and list declarations, used s32 for operand_count/d0/d1, and changed i from u32 to int.
In the modulo bytecode case, removed the f1 temporary and passed the remaining stack value directly into fmodf after HSD_SListRemove.
Removed extra nested braces around the fabsf_bitwise/atan2f RAD_TO_DEG angle calculation while preserving the explicit ±90.0F zero-x special case.
Simplified case 0x32 boolean logic from nested bool temporaries into the direct expression (d0 == 0 && d1 != 0) || (d0 != 0 && d1 == 0).
Verification listed by the PR author: ninja -j8, clang-format pre-commit on the touched files, and full pre-commit on the touched files.
smart_moves: Used project typedefs f32 and s32 consistently across implementation, public header, and robj.c extern declaration, avoiding mixed float/int/u32 prototypes.
Made fmodf codegen more controllable by naming the operands, introducing an explicit long long quotient, and separating the early-return branch from the remainder calculation.
Preserved semantics in stack VM cases while changing temporary lifetime and expression shape, which is often decisive for matching Metrowerks output.
Collapsed a convoluted nested boolean construction into a direct xor-like zero/nonzero expression, reducing source noise while still matching.
Kept the angle edge-case behavior explicit with fabsf_bitwise(f0) == 0.0F and ±90.0F instead of relying only on atan2f.
Backed the change with both local build/pre-commit verification and the decomp.dev matching report.
decomp_lessons: In baselib signatures, fixed-width/platform typedefs such as f32 and s32 can matter for matching and should be kept consistent between .c files, headers, and extern declarations.
For evaluator/bytecode-stack code, matching may depend on whether a popped stack operand is stored in a named temporary or read inline after HSD_SListRemove.
A helper function written as a compact ternary can produce different codegen than an explicit if plus assigned integer quotient; use explicit temporaries when the target code suggests them.
Extra lexical scopes and bool temporaries are not always needed for matching; simpler source can match when it better reflects the compiler's optimized control flow.
When changing a function's apparent type signature for a match, search for hand-written extern declarations as well as the public header.
Automated AI/Codex renaming can introduce questionable churn; keep renames minimal unless they are supported by matching or project conventions.
naming_conventions: ByteCodeVal remains the union used to reinterpret stack data as f32 or integer data.
Use project aliases f32 and s32 in HSD_ByteCodeEval declarations rather than raw float and int where the matched source expects platform typedefs.
The rewritten fmodf helper uses operand names divisor and dividend, matching the revised calculation order.
Pointer formatting follows project clang-format style such as HSD_SList* stack rather than HSD_SList *stack.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Introduced long long quotient in fmodf and assigned quotient = dividend / divisor before returning dividend - divisor * quotient.
Changed fmodf from return __fabsf(x) > __fabsf(m) ? x : ... to an explicit if branch, which likely helped reproduce target branch and temporary behavior.
Removed the f1 local in the modulo case and called fmodf(f0, ((ByteCodeVal*) &stack->data)->f) directly to alter operand lifetime/register allocation.
Changed local signedness and declaration order in HSD_ByteCodeEval, including s32 operand_count and s32 d0/d1.
Flattened nested atan2 calculation blocks without changing the fabsf_bitwise zero special case.
Replaced nested bool c/val control flow with a direct zero/nonzero boolean expression for case 0x32.
Kept HSD_ASSERT and HSD_ASSERTMSG stack checks intact around stack operations.

review_feedback.text: idk why codex insisted on renaming this everywhere. can clean up if preferred
review_feedback.lesson: The only human review comment in the slice was a self-note questioning Codex-driven renaming churn; there is no evidence of an external reviewer requiring cleanup.

review_feedback.text: GALE01 report: HSD_ByteCodeEval changed from 99.92% to 100.00%; matched code +0.15%, +5640 bytes overall.
review_feedback.lesson: The automated report confirms the PR achieved the intended function match.

follow_up_queries: HSD_ByteCodeEval f32 args s32 nb_args robj extern
bytecode.c case 0x32 d0 d1 boolean xor
